info.title: ChEMBL Data REST API
info.description: ChEMBL is a manually curated database of bioactive molecules with drug-like properties, maintained by the EMBL-EBI. This REST API provides programmatic access to bioactive molecules, drug targets, bioactivity measurements, approved drugs, assay data, and clinical trial compound information. ChEMBL_37 contains over 2.9 million distinct compounds, 24.5 million activities, and 18,552 targets.

tags.name: Activity
tags.description: Activity values recorded in assays, linking molecules to targets with quantitative bioactivity measurements.

tags.name: Assay
tags.description: Experimental protocols and result details from source documents and datasets.

tags.name: Molecule
tags.description: Chemical molecules including properties, structural representations, synonyms, and drug-like properties.

tags.name: Target
tags.description: Protein and non-protein targets defined in assays.

tags.name: Drug
tags.description: Approved drug information including applicants, patent numbers, and research codes.

tags.name: Drug Indication
tags.description: Drug-disease associations with clinical trial and regulatory references.

tags.name: Drug Warning
tags.description: Safety information for withdrawn or black-box warned drugs.

tags.name: Mechanism
tags.description: Mechanism of action information for approved drugs.

tags.name: Compound Record
tags.description: Occurrence of a compound in a specific source document.

tags.name: Document
tags.description: Source documents and datasets from which assay data is derived.

tags.name: ATC Classification
tags.description: WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical classification for drugs.

tags.name: Binding Site
tags.description: Target binding site definitions used in assays.

tags.name: Biotherapeutic
tags.description: Biotherapeutic molecules including HELM notation and sequence data.

tags.name: Cell Line
tags.description: Cell line information used in assays.

tags.name: Similarity
tags.description: Molecule similarity searching using Tanimoto coefficient.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: API operational status and ChEMBL database version information.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of activity records. Activities link molecules to targets with quantitative bioactivity measurements (IC50, Ki, EC50, etc.).

parameters.name: molecule_chembl_id
parameters.description: Filter by ChEMBL ID of the compound

parameters.name: target_chembl_id
parameters.description: Filter by ChEMBL ID of the target

parameters.name: assay_chembl_id
parameters.description: Filter by ChEMBL ID of the assay

parameters.name: standard_type
parameters.description: Filter by activity type (e.g., IC50, Ki, EC50, Kd)

parameters.name: standard_value__lte
parameters.description: Filter by standard value less than or equal to

parameters.name: standard_value__gte
parameters.description: Filter by standard value greater than or equal to

parameters.name: assay_type
parameters.description: Filter by assay type (B=Binding, F=Functional, A=ADME, T=Toxicity, P=Physicochemical, U=Unclassified)

200.description: Successful response with paginated list of activities

get.description: Retrieve a single activity record by its unique activity ID.
Full-text search for activities using query string.
Retrieve a paginated list of assays. Each assay describes an experimental protocol and associated results from source documents.
Retrieve a single assay by its ChEMBL ID.
Retrieve a paginated list of molecules. Includes chemical properties, structural representations (SMILES, InChI), synonyms, and drug-like properties.

parameters.name: max_phase
parameters.description: Filter by maximum clinical development phase (4 = approved)

parameters.name: therapeutic_flag
parameters.description: Filter for molecules with therapeutic applications

get.description: Retrieve a single molecule record by its ChEMBL ID.
Full-text search for molecules by name, synonym, or other text fields.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query string (molecule name, synonym, etc.)

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of targets. Targets can be single proteins, protein complexes, organisms, cell lines, or other biological entities.

parameters.name: target_type
parameters.description: Filter by target type (e.g., SINGLE PROTEIN, PROTEIN COMPLEX)

get.description: Retrieve a single target record by its ChEMBL ID.
Full-text search for targets by name or other text fields.
Retrieve a paginated list of approved drugs with information on applicants, patent numbers, research codes, and regulatory data.
Retrieve drug-disease associations with clinical trial phase information and regulatory references.

parameters.name: max_phase_for_ind
parameters.description: Filter by maximum clinical phase for the indication

get.description: Retrieve safety information for drugs that have been withdrawn or carry black-box warnings.
Retrieve mechanism of action information for approved drugs, including target, action type, and selectivity.
Search for molecules similar to a given SMILES string using Tanimoto coefficient. Returns molecules above the specified similarity threshold.

parameters.name: smiles
parameters.description: SMILES string of the query molecule (URL-encoded)

200.description: Similar molecules above the specified threshold

get.description: Search for molecules containing a given SMILES substructure.

parameters.name: smiles
parameters.description: SMILES string representing the substructure query (URL-encoded)

get.description: Retrieve an SVG structural image for a given molecule.

parameters.name: ignore_coords
parameters.description: Ignore existing coordinates and recalculate layout

get.description: Retrieve WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification records.
Retrieve target binding site definitions used in assays.
Retrieve cell line information used in assays.
Identify the entity type (molecule, assay, target, document, etc.) for a given ChEMBL ID.
Retrieve source documents and datasets from which assay data is derived.
Retrieve compound records representing the occurrence of a compound in a specific document.
Retrieve gene ontology slim classifications used to annotate targets.

200.description: Paginated list of GO slim entries

get.description: Retrieve metabolic pathway data with substrate, product, and enzyme references.

parameters.name: substrate_molecule_chembl_id
parameters.description: Filter by substrate molecule ChEMBL ID

get.description: Retrieve hierarchical protein family classification information for targets.
Retrieve tissue classification information.
Retrieve cross-reference source definitions for external chemical and biological databases.
Retrieve API operational status and ChEMBL database version information including record counts.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Number of records to return per page (default 20, max 1000)

offset.name: offset
offset.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

limit.description: Number of records returned per page

next.description: URL for the next page of results

previous.description: URL for the previous page of results

total_count.description: Total number of records matching the query

chembl_release_date.description: Release date of current ChEMBL version

Activity.description: A bioactivity measurement linking a compound to a target through an assay

activity_id.description: Unique ID for the activity record

assay_type.description: Assay type code (B=Binding, F=Functional, A=ADME, T=Toxicity, P=Physicochemical, U=Unclassified)

canonical_smiles.description: Canonical SMILES representation of the compound

document_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the source document

molecule_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the tested compound

pchembl_value.description: Negative log10 of the molar activity value (-log10 of standard value in M)

relation.description: Relationship qualifier for the activity value (=, >, <, >=, <=, ~)

standard_text_value.description: Standardized text value for non-numeric activities

standard_type.description: Standardized activity type (e.g., IC50, Ki, EC50, Kd)

standard_units.description: Standardized units of the activity measurement

target_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the biological target

target_pref_name.description: Preferred name of the biological target

target_tax_id.description: NCBI taxonomy ID of the target organism

type.description: Original activity type as reported in the source document

units.description: Original units as reported in the source document

value.description: Original numeric or text value as reported in the source document

Assay.description: An experimental assay describing the protocol used to measure bioactivity

assay_cell_type.description: Cell type used in the assay

assay_chembl_id.description: Unique ChEMBL ID for the assay

assay_description.description: Description of the assay as curated from the source document

assay_organism.description: Organism from which the assay target comes

assay_strain.description: Strain of organism used in the assay

assay_subcellular_fraction.description: Subcellular fraction used in the assay

assay_tax_id.description: NCBI taxonomy ID of the assay organism

assay_tissue.description: Tissue from which the assay is derived

document_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the source document

relationship_type.description: Relationship between the assay and its molecular target

src_assay_id.description: Assay ID from the source database

target_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the assay target

variant_sequence.description: Sequence mutation information for variant assays

MoleculeProperties.description: Calculated physicochemical properties of a molecule

full_molformula.description: Molecular formula including all salt and solvent components

full_mwt.description: Molecular weight of parent plus all salt and solvent components

hba.description: Number of hydrogen bond acceptors (Lipinski definition)

hba_lipinski.description: Number of hydrogen bond acceptors (Lipinski HBA definition)

hbd.description: Number of hydrogen bond donors (Lipinski definition)

hbd_lipinski.description: Number of hydrogen bond donors (Lipinski HBD definition)

molecular_species.description: Classification of the molecular species (ACID, BASE, NEUTRAL, ZWITTERION)

mw_freebase.description: Molecular weight of the freebase/parent form

num_lipinski_ro5_violations.description: Number of violations of Lipinski's Rule of Five

num_ro5_violations.description: Number of Lipinski Rule of Five violations

ro3_pass.description: Indicates whether the compound satisfies the Rule of Three (fragment-like)

Molecule.description: A chemical molecule in the ChEMBL database

molecule_chembl_id.description: Unique ChEMBL ID for the molecule

max_phase.description: Maximum clinical development phase (4 = approved, null = not progressed)

molecule_type.description: Type of molecule (Small molecule, Protein, Antibody, Oligosaccharide, Oligonucleotide, Cell, Unknown)

first_in_class.description: First in class flag (1=first compound of its class)

black_box_warning.description: Black box warning flag (1=has black box warning)

withdrawn_flag.description: Withdrawn from market in at least one country

molecule_synonyms.description: List of synonyms and trade names

TargetComponent.description: A component (protein sequence) of a target

relationship.description: Relationship of this component to the target

target_chembl_id.description: Unique ChEMBL ID for the target

target_type.description: Target type (e.g., SINGLE PROTEIN, PROTEIN COMPLEX, ORGANISM, CELL-LINE)

tax_id.description: NCBI taxonomy ID of the target organism

species_group_flag.description: Indicates target represents a group of species

target_components.description: Protein components making up this target

cross_references.description: Cross-references to external databases (UniProt, Ensembl, etc.)

Drug.description: An approved drug with regulatory and patent information

molecule_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the drug molecule

DrugIndication.description: Association between a drug and a disease indication

drugind_id.description: Unique ID for the drug indication record

efo_term.description: EFO ontology term for the disease/indication

max_phase_for_ind.description: Maximum clinical phase reached for this specific indication

mesh_heading.description: MeSH heading for the disease indication

DrugWarning.description: Safety warning information for a drug

warning_id.description: Unique ID for the warning record

warning_type.description: Type of warning (e.g., BLACK BOX WARNING, WITHDRAWN)

Mechanism.description: Mechanism of action for an approved drug

mec_id.description: Unique ID for the mechanism record

action_type.description: Action type (e.g., INHIBITOR, AGONIST, ANTAGONIST, MODULATOR)

direct_interaction.description: Indicates a direct interaction with the target

disease_efficacy.description: Indicates the mechanism is responsible for clinical efficacy

mechanism_of_action.description: Free-text description of the mechanism of action

target_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the mechanism target

Document.description: A source document (journal article, dataset, patent) for assay data

document_chembl_id.description: Unique ChEMBL ID for the document

pubmed_id.description: PubMed ID if from a journal article

CompoundRecord.description: Occurrence of a compound in a specific source document

compound_name.description: Compound name as listed in the source document

level1.description: First level ATC code (Anatomical main group)

who_name.description: WHO recommended name for the substance

BindingSite.description: A binding site on a target

site_id.description: Unique ID for the binding site

CellLine.description: A cell line used in assays

cell_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the cell line

GoSlim.description: Gene Ontology slim term for target classification

Metabolism.description: Drug metabolism data linking substrate to metabolite via enzyme

drug_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the drug being metabolized

enzyme_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the metabolizing enzyme

substrate_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL ID of the substrate molecule

ProteinClass.description: Protein family classification in the hierarchy
